outcome_contract.why: The startup loop has no step that requires checking how every screen in the app actually looks when rendered — both in light and dark mode. Issues like invisible text in dark mode, clashing colours, and text that is too small are only caught by doing this audit manually. Adding a screen-by-screen rendered audit to the loop means these problems get found automatically for every product, every time, before launch.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: The startup loop includes a required rendered UI screen audit at S9B that covers every screen in both light and dark mode, with findings saved as an artifact and blocking issues preventing advance.

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A — gate does not touch rendered UI
Block message must clearly tell operator to run `/tools-ui-contrast-sweep` and set `Business: <BIZ>` in the report frontmatter manually. Advance packet includes `ui_sweep_gate_status`.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Block message references slug convention — less precise, more confusing
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Plan must define the exact block message strings (3 cases: no artifact, stale/missing-Business, S1 blockers or Routes=0)

engineering_coverage.applicable: Filesystem-only; no auth, no untrusted input
Gate result added to advance packet (same pattern as GATE-LAUNCH-SEC). `ui_sweep_gate`, `ui_sweep_gate_status`, `ui_sweep_report` fields.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No additional logging needed; advance packet is the audit trail

engineering_coverage.applicable: 7 unit test cases: no artifact, stale artifact, missing Business field, Routes-Tested: 0, S1-Blockers > 0, clean pass, Status: In-progress
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Fewer test cases (no Business field check); but slug-pattern matching needs its own test cases for false-positive/negative edge cases
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Plan must include a test task with explicit fixture files

engineering_coverage.applicable: Sweep report frontmatter gains `Business: <BIZ>` field. Report template updated. Existing artifacts without field = hard block.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No schema change — brittle by design
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Template change is in scope; plan must update `report-template.md` as prerequisite

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Grace period removed. Block message must explain how to re-run.

stage_payload.decision_summary: Three decisions require resolution before planning can decompose tasks: 1. **Business-scoping mechanism** — How does `GATE-UI-SWEEP-01` know a sweep artifact belongs to the business being advanced? Two viable options remain after Option A (business-agnostic glob) was ruled out by the fact-find. 2. **Degraded-mode gate behaviour** — What happens when a sweep artifact exists but has `Routes-Tested: 0` (auth-blocked, token-only analysis)? The operator wants a rendered screen-by-screen audit; the gate must be fail-closed on non-rendered sweeps. 3. **Rollout approach** — Does the new hard gate appl
goals: Decisive choice on business-scoping mechanism (Option B vs C) with rejected option documented.
Decisive choice on degraded-mode gate policy.
Decisive choice on rollout (immediate hard gate vs. grace period).
Planning handoff with concrete sequencing constraints and validation implications.
non_goals: Re-litigating whether to add the gate (operator mandate confirmed).
Introducing a new loop stage (`S9C`) — integration targets existing S9B.
constraints: Gate must remain filesystem-only at advance time.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Gate must not allow one product's sweep to satisfy another product's advance
23+ existing sweep artifacts have no `Business:` field — gate must not silently reject all history
Fewer moving parts = lower build risk and easier rollback
Block messages must tell the operator exactly what to run next
Gate logic regression risk; unit tests required
Auth-blocked / token-only sweeps must not pass the rendered-screen requirement

options_considered.Description: Require sweep artifact slug to contain the business identifier as an infix (e.g., `*brik*`, `*pipe*`). Gate globs `docs/audits/contrast-sweeps/*<BIZ-lower>*`.
options_considered.Upside: No change to sweep artifact schema or report template.
options_considered.Downside: BIZ identifiers like `BRIK` are short and may appear coincidentally in unrelated slugs (e.g., `brikette-ci-release` is BRIK but `xa-b-branding` could accidentally match `b`). Also requires all operators to follow slug-naming convention — convention not currently enforced.
options_considered.Key risks: False positives (wrong product's sweep matching) or false negatives (correctly-named sweep missed by too-strict pattern)

options_considered.Description: Add `Business: <BIZ>` to the sweep report frontmatter template; operator sets this field manually when running the sweep. Gate reads this field explicitly. Missing field = hard block.
options_considered.Upside: Unambiguous. No slug-pattern guessing. Explicitly scoped. Consistent with GATE-LAUNCH-SEC pattern of reading structured report fields.
options_considered.Downside: Requires changes to `report-template.md` (frontmatter) and `tools-ui-contrast-sweep/SKILL.md` (document required field + update loop-position). Existing sweeps (pre-gate) lack the field and hard-block — forced migration for any business at S9B.
options_considered.Key risks: Legacy artifact forced-migration: operators must re-run sweeps before advancing S9B

options_considered.Description: Hard-block when `Routes-Tested: 0`. Advisory warn when `Routes-Tested > 0` but low (e.g., 1–2 routes).
options_considered.Upside: Fail-closed on the core requirement (rendered screens). Matches operator intent.
options_considered.Downside: Operators with auth-blocked products must resolve auth before advancing S9B — may require additional prep work.
options_considered.Key risks: Auth-blocked products cannot produce a rendered sweep; if this is a structural blocker, it surfaces early.

options_considered.Downside: Undermines the core requirement: a token-only analysis is not a rendered screen audit. S1 blockers visible only in token analysis may still exist in rendered state.
options_considered.Key risks: Gate value is degraded; operator intent is not satisfied.

options_considered.Description: Immediate hard gate. Gate applies from the moment the files are merged.
options_considered.Upside: Clean, simple, consistent with operator statement ("blocking issues preventing advance").
options_considered.Downside: Mid-loop businesses hit a new hard block at their next S9B advance attempt.
options_considered.Key risks: Operator friction if a business is at S9B with no sweep artifact.

options_considered.Description: 30-day advisory window (warn, not block) then auto-harden.
options_considered.Downside: Adds complexity: requires a date check or a flag to track advisory vs. hard mode. Contradicts operator's stated intent.
options_considered.Key risks: Advisory mode may be ignored; the window becomes permanent by operator inaction.
options_considered.Viable?: No — adds complexity without proportionate benefit

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option C for business-scoping (`Business:` frontmatter field), D1 for degraded-mode (hard-block on `Routes-Tested: 0`), R1 for rollout (immediate hard gate).
chosen_approach.Plan task ordering: `report-template.md` change must land before any gate test that requires `Business:` field.
chosen_approach.**`Business: ` population mechanism is manual-frontmatter, not a CLI flag.** `tools-ui-contrast-sweep` has no `--business` flag and the chosen approach does not add one. Operators manually set `Business: <BIZ>` in the report frontmatter when running the sweep. The SKILL.md must document this as a required field. All references to `--business` as a CLI argument are incorrect and must not appear in planning tasks or block messages.
chosen_approach.**Option D2 (warn on Routes-Tested: 0)** — Rejected. Undermines the operator-stated requirement for a rendered screen audit. Makes the gate semantically misleading.

rejected_approaches: **Option A (business-agnostic glob)** — Eliminated. Cross-product contamination risk. 23+ unrelated slugs already in `docs/audits/contrast-sweeps/`.

**Option B (slug-infix matching)** — Rejected. Brittle for short identifiers (`BRIK`, `PIPE`). No slug-naming convention is currently enforced. Prone to false positives/negatives without operator convention guarantee.
**Option D2 (warn on Routes-Tested: 0)** — Rejected. Undermines the operator-stated requirement for a rendered screen audit. Makes the gate semantically misleading.
**Option R2 (30-day advisory window)** — Rejected. Adds implementation complexity, is inconsistent with GATE-LAUNCH-SEC precedent, and may be ignored by operators.

validation_implications: Task 1 must land before Task 5 (tests need the template schema as contract reference).
Task 2 must land before Task 3 (gate implementation before spec version comment that references it).
Tasks 1–4 are documentation/config only (markdown and YAML); no app code changes in those tasks.

Task 5 adds TypeScript/Jest code to the `scripts` package. `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` must pass for the scripts package — these run in CI. The reference implementation is `scripts/src/startup-loop/__tests__/s6b-gate-simulation.test.ts`.
`scripts/check-startup-loop-contracts.sh` should be run after Task 3 to verify spec integrity.
sequencing_constraints: Tasks 1 → 2 → 3 → 4/5 (4 and 5 can be parallel; 1–3 are sequential).
Task 4 (alignment docs) can be done in a single commit alongside Task 3 if all files are clean.

areas.label: S9B stage execution
areas.current: Operator runs `/lp-launch-qa`, optionally `/lp-design-qa`. No rendered UI audit required or checked.
areas.future: 1. Operator runs `/tools-ui-contrast-sweep` and manually sets `Business: <BIZ>` in the report frontmatter (produces artifact at `docs/audits/contrast-sweeps/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>/contrast-uniformity-report.md`). 2. Operator runs `/lp-launch-qa --business <BIZ>`. 3. Operator optionally runs `/lp-design-qa`. 4. Operator runs `startup-loop advance`. Gate checks both GATE-LAUNCH-SEC and GATE-UI-SWEEP-01.
areas.steady: lp-launch-qa and lp-design-qa unchanged. GATE-LAUNCH-SEC unchanged. Advance packet structure extended (new fields), not replaced.
areas.seams: Ordering is operator-driven; gate doesn't enforce which step runs first. Plan must document the recommended execution order in startup-loop docs. No CLI flag added to tools-ui-contrast-sweep.

areas.label: S9B→SIGNALS advance gate
areas.current: Single gate: GATE-LAUNCH-SEC (security domain of launch QA report).
areas.future: Two gates enforced: (1) GATE-LAUNCH-SEC — unchanged; (2) GATE-UI-SWEEP-01 — checks for a sweep artifact ≤30 days old, `Business: <BIZ>` present, `Status: Complete`, `Routes-Tested > 0`, `S1-Blockers = 0`. Both must pass for advance.
areas.seams: Block message for GATE-UI-SWEEP-01 must be actionable. Three block cases: (a) no artifact found, (b) stale/missing-Business-field artifact, (c) S1 blockers > 0 or Routes-Tested = 0.

areas.label: Sweep artifact schema
areas.current: `Type, Status, Audit-Date, Target-URL, Standard, Breakpoints-Tested, Modes-Tested, Routes-Tested, Issues-Total, S1-Blockers, S2-Major, S3-Minor`. No `Business:` field.
areas.future: `report-template.md` gains `Business: <BIZ>` as a required frontmatter field. Sweep invocations produce artifacts with this field. Gate reads it.
areas.steady: All other frontmatter fields unchanged. JSON sidecar schema unchanged.
areas.seams: Existing sweep artifacts (23+ reports) lack `Business:` field. If any business is currently at S9B with a recent sweep, they must re-run the sweep. Plan block message must explain this.

areas.label: `tools-ui-contrast-sweep` SKILL.md
areas.current: Self-declares "Loop position: S9C (Parallel Sweep)" — S9C does not exist in loop-spec.
areas.future: Loop position updated to "S9B secondary skill, required before S9B→SIGNALS advance."
areas.steady: All sweep workflow, output paths, severity model unchanged.

areas.label: `loop-spec.yaml`
areas.current: spec_version 3.15.0. S9B: `skill: /lp-launch-qa`, `secondary_skills: [/lp-design-qa]`. GATE-LAUNCH-SEC comment block present.
areas.future: spec_version 3.16.0. S9B: `secondary_skills: [/lp-design-qa, /tools-ui-contrast-sweep]`. New GATE-UI-SWEEP-01 comment block added to S9B stage. New changelog entry at top.

areas.seams: VC-02 alignment: startup-loop SKILL.md, cmd-advance.md summary line, and any doc referencing spec_version must be updated.
